# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EC2 instances with attached EBS volumes for application data

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: VPC for the EC2 instances

  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: Subnet for the EC2 instances

  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
      - t3.xlarge
      - t3.2xlarge
      - m5.large
      - m5.xlarge
      - m5.2xlarge
    Description: EC2 instance type

  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: EC2 Key Pair for SSH access

  LatestAmiId:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<AWS::EC2::Image::Id>
    Default: /aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/al2023-ami-kernel-default-x86_64
    Description: Latest Amazon Linux 2023 AMI

  InstanceCount:
    Type: Number
    Default: 1
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 10
    Description: Number of EC2 instances to launch

  RootVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 20
    MinValue: 8
    MaxValue: 1024
    Description: Root EBS volume size in GiB

  DataVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 50
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 16384
    Description: Data EBS volume size in GiB

  DataVolumeType:
    Type: String
    Default: gp3
    AllowedValues:
      - gp3
      - gp2
      - io1
      - io2
      - st1
      - sc1
      - standard
    Description: Data EBS volume type

Resources:
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for application EC2 instances
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

  InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore

  InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref InstanceRole

  EC2Instance0:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref LatestAmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref InstanceProfile
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: !Ref RootVolumeSize
            VolumeType: gp3
            DeleteOnTermination: true
            Encrypted: false
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: !Sub |
          #!/bin/bash -xe
          dnf update -y
          yum install -y nvme-cli || true
          mkdir -p /data
          echo "Instance 0 ready" > /data/instance.txt
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-app-0'

  DataVolume0:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Volume
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !GetAtt EC2Instance0.AvailabilityZone
      Size: !Ref DataVolumeSize
      VolumeType: !Ref DataVolumeType
      Encrypted: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-data-0'

  DataVolumeAttachment0:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment
    Properties:
      InstanceId: !Ref EC2Instance0
      VolumeId: !Ref DataVolume0
      Device: /dev/sdf

Conditions:
  CreateAdditionalInstances: !Not [!Equals [!Ref InstanceCount, 1]]

Outputs:
  InstanceId0:
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance0
  InstancePrivateIp0:
    Value: !GetAtt EC2Instance0.PrivateIp
  DataVolumeId0:
    Value: !Ref DataVolume0
